strategies.name: Interleave-order correctness under sparse activity
strategies.target: Mutate existing find-last conditional-scalar-assignment test to force VF=4, IC=4 and create masks where only part1/part2 are active in some vector iterations, part3 inactive.
strategies.rationale: The new semantics rely on chaining ExtractLastActive as (default, data0,mask0, data1,mask1, ...). If part order or operand appending is wrong in unrollBlock, loops with activity in non-final parts can miscompile.
strategies.expected_issue: Wrong live-out value chosen from an earlier part (or default) instead of the last active part when IC>2.

strategies.name: Scalar-VF interleave path (VF=1, IC>N)
strategies.target: Mutate a find-last test to run with -force-vector-width=1 -force-vector-interleave=4 and check for chained scalar selects and correct resume value/live-out.
strategies.rationale: generate() now has a scalar path for ExtractLastActive using select(mask,data,result). This is new for multi-part chaining and can be wrong if unroll did not append all part operands or if part order is reversed.
strategies.expected_issue: Incorrect scalar fallback in middle block for VF=1, IC>1 (wrong select chaining across parts or missed part operands).

strategies.name: Tail-fold + interleave + find-last
strategies.target: Mutate existing VPlan interleave conditional-scalar-assignment test with -prefer-predicate-over-epilogue=predicate-dont-vectorize and IC=4/8, verifying extract-last-active operands include all part mask/data pairs and correct middle.block value flow.
strategies.rationale: Patch depends on AnyOf + per-part mask select logic to ensure inactive later parts become all-false while preserving last active part state. Predicated tail loops stress this heavily.
strategies.expected_issue: Tail-folded predicated vector loop produces wrong final value when higher parts are inactive, due to incorrect mask propagation per part.

strategies.name: Multiple find-last reductions in same loop
strategies.target: Mutate a test to have two or more conditional scalar assignments (different element types/arrays) in one loop with IC=4, checking both live-outs in middle block and scalar epilogue phis.
strategies.rationale: The fix changed VPWidenPHI clone/remap behavior and added mask phis for find-last. Multiple independent find-last recurrences can expose operand-remap bugs or accidental sharing.
strategies.expected_issue: Cross-wired phi/backedge operands after unroll (assertion or wrong value) when multiple FindLast reductions coexist.

strategies.name: Type/arity robustness for variadic ExtractLastActive
strategies.target: Mutate conditional-scalar-assignment tests to include pointer and floating find-last recurrences under forced IC=4 (and masked-memops test knob), checking no verifier/assert failures and correct final select behavior.
strategies.rationale: ExtractLastActive is now variadic and assumes operand[0]=default scalar and then data/mask pairs. There is no explicit arity/type assert in generate(). Edge typed recurrences can reveal latent mismatch.
strategies.expected_issue: Crash/assert/type mismatch in VPlan/IR generation for ExtractLastActive when type assumptions are violated (especially pointer/non-integer recurrences) or malformed operand pairing.

reason_thou:
1. Fix Understanding
- This patch enables interleaving for FindLast-style conditional scalar assignments in LoopVectorize, removing the previous hard disable (IC forced to 1).
- Core semantic change: VPInstruction::ExtractLastActive changed from fixed arity (data, mask, default) to variadic form: (default, data0, mask0, data1, mask1, ...), intended to combine UF parts.
- Construction updated accordingly in handleFindLastReductions() to create ExtractLastActive with default first.
- Unrolling updated to append per-part data/mask operands for ExtractLastActive in unrollBlock().
- Codegen updated in VPInstruction::generate():
  - vector VF: chain llvm.experimental.vector.extract_last_active calls across parts;
  - scalar VF: chain scalar select(mask, data, result).
- Auxiliary updates:
  - Added hidden test knob force-target-supports-masked-memory-ops.
  - VPWidenPHIRecipe clone now tolerates null underlying PHI (needed by synthetic mask-phi recipe).
  - unrollRecipeByUF skips immediate remap for VPWidenPHI clones, deferring phi fixups.

2. Formal Semantic Modeling
- Before fix (effective behavior):
  - FindLast loops could vectorize but were not interleaved when FindLast reduction existed (IC forced to 1).
  - ExtractLastActive conceptually computed: ELA(data, mask, default) for one part only.
- After fix:
  - Interleaving allowed for FindLast.
  - Result semantics for UF parts:
    R0 = default
    R1 = ELA(data0, mask0, R0)
    R2 = ELA(data1, mask1, R1)
    ...
    RUF = ELA(dataUF-1, maskUF-1, RUF-1)
  - For scalar VF, ELA is replaced by select(mask_i, data_i, R_prev), equivalent over scalar lanes.
- Concrete instantiation:
  - default = -1, VF=4, UF=4.
  - masks per part:
    p0=[0,1,0,0], p1=[0,0,0,0], p2=[0,0,1,0], p3=[0,0,0,0]
  - data lane chosen by p0 is d1, by p2 is d10 (global position).
  - Expected final = d10 (last active across concatenated parts), not d1 and not -1.
- Conditions where before/after differ:
  - Any case with interleave factor >1 and FindLast recurrence (previously disallowed).

3. Assumptions and Preconditions Identified
- A1: ExtractLastActive operands are well-formed: odd count >=3, operand0 is scalar default, remaining operands are (data, mask) pairs.
- A2: Data/mask pair types are consistent across all parts; default type equals data element type.
- A3: Unroll appends part operands in execution order (part0..partN), preserving concatenation semantics.
- A4: Mask update logic (AnyOf/select) correctly carries forward through inactive parts, so later inactive parts do not clobber last-active state.
- A5: Deferred VPWidenPHI operand remap is sufficient for all cloned widened phis involved in this transform.

4. Potential Cases to Break Assumptions
- Break A3: Part operand appending order wrong or missing some parts -> returns value from wrong part.
- Break A4: In predicated/tail-folded loops, inactive high parts may incorrectly keep stale masks or data, causing wrong final middle.block value.
- Break A1/A2: Variadic opcode with type/arity mismatch could trigger verifier/assert/OOB in generate() (no explicit arity assert there).
- Break A5: Multiple find-last recurrences increase phi clone/remap complexity; could cross-wire incoming values.
- Scalar corner of A3/A4: VF=1 with IC>1 relies entirely on new scalar select chain; errors may not appear in vector-VF tests.

5. Test Strategies
- Focused on stressing assumptions above with existing LoopVectorize tests by mutation:
  1) sparse active masks across mid parts,
  2) VF=1 IC>1 path,
  3) tail-folded predication,
  4) multiple concurrent find-last recurrences,
  5) type/arity robustness via non-i32 find-last forms.
- Expected observable failures: wrong live-out, incorrect middle.block resume value, missing/incorrect extract-last-active chains, verifier/assert failures, or unexpected fallback (no interleave when expected).

define i64 @findlast_arith(i64 %N, i64 %limit) {
entry:
  br label %loop

loop:
  %iv = phi i64 [0, %entry], [%iv.next, %loop]
  %rdx = phi i64 [-1, %entry], [%sel, %loop]
  %cond = icmp ult i64 %iv, %limit
  %sel = select i1 %cond, i64 %iv, i64 %rdx
  %iv.next = add nuw nsw i64 %iv, 1
  %exit = icmp eq i64 %iv.next, %N
  br i1 %exit, label %exitbb, label %loop

exitbb:
  ret i64 %sel
}

define i64 @main(i32 %argc, ptr %argv) {
entry:
  %r = call i64 @findlast_arith(i64 37, i64 23)
  ret i64 %r
}
